title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return to play against the Washington Wizards after sitting out Thursday for injury management.
The Celtics are managing Tatum's minutes following a right ruptured Achilles, limiting him to 27 minutes per game.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable for the game, while Nikola Vucevic is out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is now streaming on Netflix, featuring a soldier dealing with PTSD.
'Zootopia 2', featuring Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin, is available for streaming on Disney+.
HBO Max's new show 'Rooster', starring Steve Carell, streams, blending comedy and heartfelt storytelling.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots acquire former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker amid offensive line changes, trading Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Vera-Tucker missed last season with a triceps injury and has a history of significant injuries since 2021.
Morgan Moses, former teammate, influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join the Patriots for their winning culture and coaching staff.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora identifies Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders of the 2026 Boston Red Sox.
Trevor Story led the Red Sox in home runs, RBI, runs, and hits in 2025, solidifying his leadership role.
Garrett Crochet became a starter in 2024, led MLB in strikeouts in 2025, and draws comparisons to Randy Johnson.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC begins their inaugural NWSL season against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão aims for a possession-style game amid challenges of NWSL's salary cap and roster constraints.
Gotham FC, the defending NWSL champions, features US stars Rose Lavelle and Jaedyn Shaw in their lineup.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports legislation to end the biannual clock change in the state.
The proposed bill, S.2157, suggests adopting Atlantic Standard Time year-round to improve health and productivity.
Research indicates clock changes increase risks of heart attacks, strokes, and disrupt routines, but affects daylight distribution.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date for June 6 in Boston.
The 2026 event will start at Copley Square at 11 a.m. and end at Boston Common.
Mayor Michelle Wu supports the event, praising its focus on joy, inclusivity, and diversity.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released and reunited with family in Massachusetts.
Elected officials criticized the detainment, alleging the girl was used to locate her father; DHS states she was rescued from gang members.
She faces potential deportation with a notice to appear in court; her lawyer seeks to secure her legal residency.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are struggling with scoring as key players like David Pastrnak and Elias Lindholm underperform in even-strength play.
Coach Marco Sturm reshuffled lines during a 4-2 loss to the San Jose Sharks, seeking more offensive production.
Possible lineup changes include separating Pastrnak and Lindholm and possibly adding prospect James Hagens to boost scoring.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss student, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne is a visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, focusing on neurorehabilitation for trauma survivors.
He aims to support trauma survivor resilience and highlight the need for research and rehabilitation funding.
